text.title: KPI Drift Alarm Template Core Rules
text.summary: 8 testable rules with rationale + source for kpi-drift-alarm-template. Includes terminal skip-this-methodology rule.

rule.statement: Each KPI MUST have a rolling baseline of at least 4 cycles; baselines shorter than 4 cycles are auto-rejected as noisy.
rule.rationale: Statistical drift research — applies to KPI Drift Alarm Template.

rule.statement: Deviation threshold MUST specify direction (above / below / either); thresholds without direction are forbidden because drift can be benign or malignant per direction.
rule.rationale: KPI design practice — applies to KPI Drift Alarm Template.

rule.statement: Alarms MUST be two-class: warning (1 sigma deviation) + critical (2 sigma deviation); single-class alarms produce alarm fatigue.
rule.rationale: Site reliability engineering — applies to KPI Drift Alarm Template.

rule.statement: After firing, an alarm is suppressed for a defined window (default 1 cycle) to avoid storm; suppression window MUST be configured per KPI.
rule.rationale: Alert storm prevention — applies to KPI Drift Alarm Template.

rule.statement: If the same alarm fires for 3 consecutive cycles, escalation routes it to a named owner; silent re-firing is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Escalation-discipline practice — applies to KPI Drift Alarm Template.

rule.statement: Every alarm message MUST include a deep link to the underlying chart / query; alarms without data context are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Alert quality practice — applies to KPI Drift Alarm Template.

rule.statement: Every fired alarm MUST be closed with a written resolution (root cause, action taken); open alarms older than 1 cycle escalate.
rule.rationale: Incident-management practice — applies to KPI Drift Alarm Template.

rule.statement: If KPI history is shorter than 4 cycles or no on-call coverage exists, return `skipped`.
rule.rationale: Methodology applicability — applies to KPI Drift Alarm Template.
